mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easing financial conditions, lowers discount rates, and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still rising, markets infer restrictive policy will persist, which compresses valuation multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rising over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months indicates deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment acceleration is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if the signal loses significance after repeated out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in RSAFS indicates weakening nominal consumer spending and lower corporate revenue growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if declines in RSAFS do not correspond to lower 20-day SPX returns versus its own expanding baseline, or if rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and broader cyclical momentum, which should depress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO changes fail to lead 5-day SPX weakness, or if the sign of the effect reverses during independent backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent 6-month average signals housing demand deterioration and tighter credit transmission, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts contractions do not predict weaker 60-day SPX performance, or if triggered periods fail to outperform a random benchmark in statistical tests.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is rising, payroll losses and rising slack jointly confirm a labor-market deterioration that should cause negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor stress condition is not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if either proxy alone explains the effect fully.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, strong GDPC1 growth together with resilient RSAFS implies demand is still broadening and supports corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-growth GDPC1 and RSAFS regimes do not correspond to stronger 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction adds no predictive power beyond either series alone.
